description: PHIL-5 S399 challenge resolution: measure net knowledge gain over 50 sessions
challenge_criterion: If net < gross by >30%, amend to 'learn faster than decay'
method: Direct supersession count: grep lesson titles for SUPERSEDED/DEPRECATED + cross-reference superseding chains in lesson bodies. Compare with knowledge_state.py citation-recency metric.
expect: PHIL-5 S399 criterion met (decay > 30%) based on S457 citation-recency finding

knowledge_state_comparison.note: L-813: citation-recency not validity — actual false knowledge ~5-10%

diff: S399 criterion NOT MET: 6.1% actual decay, well under 30% threshold. S457 CONFIRMED based on citation-recency (32.2%) which L-813 defines as 'not validity'. Conflation error: citation-staleness != knowledge loss. PHIL-5 HOLDS by original criterion.
verdict: PHIL-5 CONFIRMED — swarm learns faster than it decays (6.1% vs 30% threshold). Citation-staleness (32.2%) is an accessibility problem, not a learning problem. S457 refinement to 'learn faster than decay' is directionally correct as a prose improvement but the 30% threshold was never breached.

measurement_discrepancy.root_cause: L-813 distinction not applied: citation-recency measures 'when last cited', not 'still valid'
